title: carya alba var. ovalis
intro: Carya alba var. ovalis, also known as the White Walnut or Eastern Black Walnut, is a deciduous tree native to eastern North America, characterized by its large, oval-shaped leaves and edible nuts. Traditionally, the bark, leaves, and nuts of this plant have been used in herbal medicine due to their antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Carya alba var. ovalis include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting the immune system, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies for various health issues. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or oil, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties and health benefits.
